circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, designed by Hermann Tilke, is a 5. 281 km track located on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi that has served as the venue for the final Formula One race of the season since 2009. The circuit represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One venue, following Bahrain and preceding Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street course that encircles Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb and holds an FIA Grade 1 classification. It serves as the venue for the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix, alongside the Supercars Championship Melbourne SuperSprint and various supporting racing events.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit originally operated as the Österreichring and staged the Austrian Grand Prix annually from 1970 to 1987. Following a redesign and renaming, the track resumed hosting Austria's Formula One race from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: Baku City Circuit is a street-based racing venue situated along Baku Boulevard in Azerbaijan, measuring 6. 003 km per lap and ranking as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The circuit first hosted the 2016 European Grand Prix, before being renamed to host the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One race and continues to stage the annual Bahrain Grand Prix alongside various other motorsport categories. The FIA Grade 1 licensed facility features multiple track configurations and has accommodated competitions ranging from drag racing and GP2 Series events to 24-hour endurance races, though Australian V8 Supercars competed there only between 2006 and 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a 4. 657 km motorsport venue featuring a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing various racing skills. The facility holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: This Belgian circuit spanning 7. 004 km near the town of Spa has been a Formula One fixture since the sport's inaugural 1950 season. The venue has hosted the Belgian Grand Prix annually since 1985, with only two interruptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km temporary street circuit that hosts the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the venue has been home to various other international motorsport competitions including sportscar championships and North American racing series.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit in China's Jiading District is primarily recognized as the home of the Chinese Grand Prix, which took place annually from 2004 until 2019 before resuming in 2024. The venue has expanded its racing calendar to include the Shanghai ePrix from 2024 onward, hosting both traditional and electric motorsport competitions.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near Silverstone in Northamptonshire, this English circuit has been the principal venue for the British Grand Prix since 1987, having first staged the race in 1948 and hosting the inaugural World Championship round in 1950. The track shared hosting duties with Aintree and Brands Hatch between 1955 and 1986 before becoming the permanent home of Britain's premier motorsport event, and currently also features on the MotoGP calendar.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, located near Budapest in Hungary, is a 4. 381 km circuit that hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months, faster than any other Formula One venue, the track was constructed outside the city after plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing circuit constructed in 1922 near Milan, representing the third purpose-built motorsport venue globally and Europe's oldest on the mainland. Home to the Italian Grand Prix continuously since 1949 (except during 1980 renovations), the track earns its "Temple of Speed" nickname from its characteristically long straights and fast-flowing corners.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue in Mie Prefecture, is operated by Honda Mobilityland and accommodates 155,000 spectators. The track hosts both Formula One and Super Formula championships and ranks among the sport's most prestigious racing destinations.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, weaving around the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its surrounding urban landscape. The track incorporates several iconic Las Vegas landmarks and hospitality venues, creating a distinctive urban racing environment for Formula One competition.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the 4. 304 km Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez honors two Mexican racing brothers who died in separate accidents. The venue resumed hosting Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix in 2015 using a revised layout, having previously staged the event during two earlier periods that concluded in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens that debuted on the Formula One calendar in 2022. Stretching 3. 363 miles with 19 corners, the track achieves qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph, making it a moderately fast layout designed specifically for Formula One racing.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco stretches 3. 337 kilometers through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, weaving around Monaco's harbor. Though officially recognized by its full name, the track is popularly known as Monte Carlo since most of its layout occupies that particular neighborhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, this 4. 259 km track hosted the resurrected Dutch Grand Prix starting in 2021 after a long absence from the Formula One calendar. The venue will conclude its F1 partnership following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Lusail International Circuit sits north of Doha in Qatar and spans 5. 419 kilometers, featuring a 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos, a 4. 309 km Brazilian circuit that opened in São Paulo during 1940, was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his 1977 fatal plane crash. The track operates in a counterclockwise direction and now serves as Pace's final resting place, while also featuring an associated kart facility named after fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue positioned along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea coastline that has hosted the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix annually since its debut in 2021, while also welcoming Formula E competition from 2025 onward.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street track located in Singapore that winds around Marina Bay and incorporates sections through the Downtown Core and Kallang planning zones.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a 4. 657-kilometer track featuring a balanced mix of lengthy straights and varied cornering sequences that test multiple driving skills. The venue, holding an FIA Grade 1 license, can accommodate 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located in Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile Grade 1 FIA facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside hosting major motorcycle and endurance racing events. The track has accommodated numerous racing series throughout its history, including IndyCar and various international sports car championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon, a Thai-British driver, currently races for Williams in Formula One under the Thai flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories throughout his 23-season career. Beyond single-seaters, he has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories with Toyota and the 2018-19 FIA World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both Formula One and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Andrea Kimi Antonelli is an Italian Formula One driver currently racing for Mercedes. Since making his debut in 2025, he has secured five Grand Prix victories.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman, a British motorsport competitor, currently races in Formula One as a member of the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabriel Bortoleto currently races for Audi in Formula One, bringing his talent to the grid after progressing through the junior motorsport categories.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from a 13-season career that includes 10 race victories. He achieved second-place finishes in the drivers' championship during 2019 and 2020 while racing for Mercedes.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto, an Argentine motorsport competitor, currently races in Formula One as part of the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan holds a reserve role with the Haas Formula One team while competing in the European Le Mans Series LMP2 category for Nielsen Racing. He has made limited Formula One appearances, participating in seven Grand Prix events during the 2024-2025 period.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: French driver Pierre Gasly currently races for Alpine in Formula One, and previously achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while competing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Hadjar is a French-Algerian motorsport competitor who races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One, representing France internationally.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, who has achieved a record-tying seven World Drivers' Championship titles alongside Michael Schumacher. He holds numerous performance records in the sport, including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in F1 history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved significant success in endurance motorsport, capturing the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans victory with Porsche in 2015.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson, a New Zealand-born motorsport competitor, currently drives for Racing Bulls in Formula One.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a driver from Monaco, currently races for Ferrari in Formula One. He finished second in the 2022 drivers' championship and has secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his nine seasons in the sport.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen, a Danish driver commonly referred to as K-Mag, competed in Formula One across two separate periods between 2014 and 2024. Currently, he races for BMW in the FIA World Endurance Championship with WRT while maintaining a part-time NASCAR Cup Series presence driving for Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has claimed 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French motorsport competitor currently racing for Haas in Formula One. The driver achieved a Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian round while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez competes in Formula One for Cadillac, bringing extensive experience from a 15-season career that includes six race victories. He finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull, establishing himself as a consistent competitor at the sport's highest level.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Australian driver Oscar Piastri races for McLaren in Formula One, where he has secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his four-season stint in the championship.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known as "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over 14 seasons from 2011 until 2024, securing eight race victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, where he has claimed seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz Vázquez de Castro currently races for Williams in Formula One, where he has claimed four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: American driver Logan Sargeant raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following earlier experience in IMSA's LMP2 class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor who currently drives for Aston Martin in Formula One racing.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who currently holds a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls in Formula One. He previously competed as a full-time driver in the series from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen races for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024. Over his 12-season Formula One career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is China's sole Formula One competitor to date, having raced in the championship between 2022 and 2024. He currently holds a reserve role with the Cadillac team on the Formula One grid.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, continuing Renault's factory involvement in Formula One until the manufacturer's departure at the end of 2025. Operating under various names including its previous Renault identity, the squad has achieved a single victory in its Alpine guise when Esteban Ocon triumphed at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, and currently runs Mercedes power units while fielding Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained an on-and-off presence in Formula One through different team structures, with the contemporary outfit operating under AMR GP Limited and competing under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker Ferrari, stands as Formula One's most enduring and accomplished competitor, having participated in every world championship since the sport's inception in 1950. The team, nicknamed "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem, remains the oldest continuously active outfit in the championship.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas in 2014, this American-licensed outfit entered Formula One competition in 2016 and continues operating under the TGR Haas F1 Team identity. Ayao Komatsu assumed the role of team principal in January 2024, succeeding Guenther Steiner who led the squad from its establishment through the previous season.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: A Swiss engineering firm established in 1970, the team initially excelled in endurance racing before joining Formula One in 1993 as an independent constructor. After three decades competing in the sport, the organization departed in 2025 as one of F1's most experienced outfits by race count, having undergone numerous transformations during its tenure. Audi acquired the team and its infrastructure in 2024 to serve as the foundation for its own Formula One entry.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited operates as a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its Woking facility in Surrey. The team functions as part of the McLaren Group, with ownership held by Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a continuous presence in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier. The current team, Mercedes-AMG Petronas, operates from Brackley under a German licence, with ownership shared between the Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff since 2022. The outfit is traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows" in racing circles.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team that began competing in 2024 as Red Bull's second constructor alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with operations in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as part of the Austrian conglomerate's dual-team Formula One structure.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that began competing in 2024 as one of two teams owned by Red Bull GmbH, alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with operations near its sister team in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as Red Bull's second constructor entry in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing competes in Formula One under an Austrian racing license from its English base, operating as one of two F1 teams within the Red Bull GmbH conglomerate. Founded in 2005 under Christian Horner's management, the team transitioned leadership to Laurent Mekies in 2025 following Horner's departure.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's previous unsuccessful racing venture. Based at their Grove facility in Oxfordshire, the team has competed in Formula One as Atlassian Williams F1 Team in 2026.
